title: Timing to Restart Direct Oral Anticoagulants After Traumatic Intracranial Haemorrhage

skipped_criteria: Inclusion 1: Informed consent and ability to comply cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Inclusion 2 (partial): 'traumatic intracranial haemorrhage in the past 1 week' — temporal constraint (within 1 week of injury) cannot be expressed in the schema.
Inclusion 3: Specific OAC type (DOAC or VKA/Warfarin) and indication (AF or VTE) cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Inclusion 4: CHA2DS2VASc score threshold (≥2 in men, ≥3 in women) is a composite clinical score not available as a schema field.
Exclusion 1: Chronic subdural haematoma subtype cannot be distinguished from other intracranial haemorrhage types in the schema.
Exclusion 2: Mechanical heart valve history is not a schema field.
Exclusion 3: Plan to start/restart anti-platelet therapy within 12 weeks is not a schema field.
Exclusion 4: Abbreviated Injury Scale score for non-head regions >3 cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 6: Hypersensitivity or contraindication to DOAC is not a schema field.
Exclusion 7: Bleeding severity making DOAC restart unsafe at 1 week cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 8: Clinical reason to restart DOAC before 4 weeks or complete within 12 weeks cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Exclusion 9: Concomitant p-gp and CYP3A4 inducers/inhibitors use is not a schema field.
Exclusion 10: Indication to stay on VKA rather than switching to DOAC (e.g., severe renal impairment) cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
